observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a.	The Bids and Awards Committee, the Municipal Engineer and all other implementing offices of the Municipality expedite the procurement and implementation process so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay; and


b.	The Local Development Council conduct a careful and well-planned identification of development projects and choose implementation ready PPAs to be funded from the 20% DF, so the desired level of efficiency is achieved.
observations.managementAction:
With these various reasons, the Engineering Office, BAC, will ensure proper and on-time im-plementation of some unimple-mented projects identified in the 20% DF.

All PPAs for CY 2022 were all implemented except for the in-stallation of CCTV to Barangays and Poblacion proper. The BAC/Engineering office will finalize the appropriate location of all CCTVs by second quarter of 2025 for the procurement process.
observations.reason:
The access [to] Bailey Bridge at Brgy. Lublub which is the only way going to the project site was damaged by several flash floods caused by heavy rains made the Bailey Bridge not passable to any four-wheel vehicle.

Some projects are for bidding, the only access way going to the project site is the Cangaranan River. The river is not safe to cross considering that it causes flash floods when it rains on the upper part. The only access going to the site is by foot which causes the non-implementation of some projects on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
 
1.	The Municipal Assessor, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and all concerned to work for the immediate titling, recording, or transfer of ownership of all land belonging to the Municipality to establish ownership and avoid risk in court litigation from the previous owners who are interested in taking back the properties.
 

2.	Secure proper and valid documentation as support to the recorded real properties in the books of the Municipality to present an accurate land account in the financial statements
observations.managementAction:
Get status of lot from DENR

Get requirements from ROD for titling of Land

Get court clearance of no-pending land registration case

Site development plan of Market Site

Further, other requirements are on-going.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented.

As per Municipal Accountant, all properties were booked as per report on the Physical Count of Land and as per General Ledger. No proper documents were turned over by the former Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality of Valderrama ensure that payments of salaries and wages is in accordance with the existing Local Budget Circular issued by the DBM particularly adopting rates equivalent to Local Government Unit level and income classification.
observations.reason: The Municipality still adopted in CY 2024 the 2nd Class Salary Schedule.

observations.recommendation: 1.14.  We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of supporting documents on claims for travelling expenses by the JO/COS workers.

2.7.     We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer compel the collectors to strictly remit at the end of each business day so that their collections will be deposited intact and regularly to the authorized depository bank to safeguard the Municipality’s funds from possible loss or improper use as provided by the Sec. 29 of the MNGAS for LGUs and Section 90 of LTOM, 2nd Edition.

3.13.  We recommended and the Management agreed to direct:
The Collecting Officer to correct and reconcile her records and reports and ensure that they are updated daily to ensure their accuracy and reliability before submitting them to the Municipal Accountant's Office for recording in the financial statements; and
The Municipal Treasurer to strictly implement the provisions in the Contract of Lease and Municipal Ordinance No. 2017-01 regarding the timely collection of stall rental payments, the imposition of surcharges for delayed payments, and the closure of stalls for stallholders with three months of delayed payments, as well as the provision of fire extinguishers.

4.9.  We recommended and the Management agreed to direct:
The Inventory and Disposal Committee conduct an investigation to gather information/details on the whereabouts of the missing parts;
The Inventory and Disposal Committee account the missing parts; and
The management enhance the internal control in property management and safeguard all its assets, whether serviceable or unserviceable.

5.12. We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant verify and reconcile the reported balances of Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts with the records of the concerned government agencies, and make a constant follow-up until resolved, through a formal letter; and
The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the premium contributions, after determination of the correct amount payable, to the concerned government agencies.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant as the Authorized Agency Officer, comply to the provision of Section 2, Rule VII of the RA No. 9679 and Application Agreement with the Pag-IBIG Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The Municipal Engineer ensures that projects funded from a lumpsum appropriation is supported with a specific authority from the Sangguniang Bayan before implementation; and
The Head of the Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, present their PPAs in the Annual Budget with specific details and information in order that additional specific authority from the Sangguniang Bayan in the implementation of projects will no longer necessary.

8.10. We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The management adhere to the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 in posting the relevant information pertaining to their PPAs through signboard or similar materials that conforms to the specifications indicated in the said Circular, in the conspicuous places within the agency premises, and in the venue where the PPA is located or carried out for all its PPAs regardless of amount or source of funds; and
The management include in the estimate of project cost of all its PPAs the cost of public notices.

We recommended that the management direct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchase of the Accountable Forms as assets using the “Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers” account following the Perpetual Inventory System, and to record to “Accountable Forms Expense” account the cost of Accountable Forms issued to end-users.

We recommended that:

a.	The Municipal Treasurer cancel the unclaimed stale checks and return the original disbursement voucher to the Municipal Accountant in order to effect the necessary adjusting entries. For stale checks in the hands of the payee, send written notices reminding them of the need to present stale checks for cancellation before replacement can be made. For checks lost in the hands of the payee, require the payee to submit a sworn statement that the check is lost; and

b.	The Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entries to record the stale checks.

We recommended that the management revert the undisbursed PDAF balances to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund of the National Government by remitting the amount to the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court Decisions.

We recommended that the management:

a.	The Inventory and Disposal Committee to take proper action on government properties that are for disposal to prevent their continued deterioration and decline in value leading to loss of possible income from disposal; and

b.	The Inventory and Disposal Committee to prepare yearly in four copies and certify correct the RPCPPE containing the complete information as prescribed by Annex 49 of the NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the management:

a.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the Source Agencies and status of implementation of all fund transfers from NGAs by securing/collating all available documents, especially the Memorandum of Agreement, relative to the Due to NGAs Account; and

b.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Subsidiary Ledgers for each fund transfer.

We recommended that the Management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations and submit the fully accomplished AAPSI to COA within the reglementary period copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 99 of RA No. 11936.

We commended the Management, especially the MENRO, in their implementation and enforcement of efficient and effective system of solid waste and garbage collection and the enforcement of policies aimed in maintaining the sanitation of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management provide all accountable officers with fireproof vaults or safe for safekeeping and the storage of cash and other valuable documents to deter theft and to protect assets against loss.

1.	Create an Inventory Committee whose members shall be temporarily relieved of all regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking for the one-time cleansing of PPE until the same is completed; (Implemented CY 2023)

2.	Prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and complete the physical inventory in three months or less, in order to come up with reliable PPE account balances, pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and  (Implemented in CY 2023)

3.	Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability

1.	Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance; and

2.	Ensure that the programs, projects, and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness be implemented within the schedule timeframes.

In view of the above, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Disposal Committee should take proper action on government properties that are for disposal to prevent their continued deterioration and decline in value leading to loss of possible income from disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile and validate the outstanding receivables and determine its collectability.  If it is impossible to collect those receivables, a request for write off be made with the Commission on   Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-01.

We recommended that the Management provide all accountable officers with fireproof vaults or safe for safekeeping and the storage of cash and other valuable documents to deter theft and to protect assets against loss.
observations.managementAction:
As per response of the Management in the inquiry of the Audit Team, out of nine (9) Aos, four (4) were provided with vaults and five (5) have no vaults yet due to lack of budget. 

As per Budget Officer, the budget for vaults is included already in the Supplemental Budget and upon approval, LGU will purchase the vaults estimated in the Month of May 2024.
observations.reason: have no vaults yet due to lack of budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed the following:
 
1.	The Municipal Assessor, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and all concerned to work for the immediate titling, recording, or transfer of ownership of all land belonging to the Municipality to establish ownership and avoid risk in court litigation from the previous owners who are interested in taking back the properties.
 


















2.	Secure proper and valid documentation as support to the recorded real properties in the books of the Municipality to present an accurate land account in the financial statements
observations.managementAction:
As per response from the Management to the inquiry by the Audit Team, Titling is on-going. The following documents were required from the management for the titling: Original Copy of Deed of Sale, Original copy of expired CAR and SB Resolution. 

Mrs. Cecilia Castillon is the person designated/tasked to process the titling of land with Designation Order dated March 21, 2024.

The management listed the following problems encountered in titling process:

No original copy of deed of sale from the owner
Need to allocate budget for payment of Capital Gain Tax/Documentary Stamp Tax
Original copy of expired CAR
SB Resolution authorizing the designated and/or Mayor’s SPA
Budget dedicated for processing of land titles of LGU-Valderrama

Not Implemented.

As per Municipal Accountant, all properties were booked as per report on the Physical Count of Land and as per General Ledger. No proper documents were turned over by the former Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason:
No original copy of deed of sale from the owner
Need to allocate budget for payment of Capital Gain Tax/Documentary Stamp Tax
Original copy of expired CAR
SB Resolution authorizing the designated and/or Mayor’s SPA
Budget dedicated for processing of land titles of LGU-Valderrama

1.	The Bids and Awards Committee, the Municipal Engineer and all other implementing offices of the Municipality expedite the procurement and implementation process so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay; and

observations.managementAction:
To speed-up the procurement and implementation of said 2 remaining projects in order to achieve “fully-implemented” status.

We are already fast-tracking the procurement and implementation of PPAs funded out of the 20% DF due to backlogs caused by the pandemic and also unforeseen calamities like typhoon, flooding and landslide. So far other projects unimplemented have already been re-appropriated or re-programmed.

The Municipal Engineer provided indexing of government projects.

Not Implemented.

To reappropriate some of prior year projects.
observations.reason: due to backlogs caused by the pandemic and also unforeseen calamities like typhoon, flooding and landslide.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

1.	Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance; and

2.	Ensure that the programs, projects, and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness be implemented within the schedule timeframes.
observations.managementAction:
1.To speed up PPAs implementation within scheduled time frame.


Not Implemented.

As per the response of the LDRRMO II, out of P8,654,787.63 CY 2022’s appropriation and prior years’  unutilized LDRRMF, P5,569,630.35 was already utilized. And the reasons for non-maximization of LDDRMF are: (1) delayed implementation of training activity (training providers were not available), (2) Delayed implementation of PPAs, and (3) Balances from implemented PPAs.
observations.reason: the reasons for non-maximization of LDDRMF are: (1) delayed implementation of training activity (training providers were not available), (2) Delayed implementation of PPAs, and (3) Balances from implemented PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile and validate the outstanding receivables and determine its collectability.  If it is impossible to collect those receivables, a request for write off be made with the Commission on   Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-01.
observations.managementAction:
As per response received from the Municipal Accountant, As of December 31, 2023, there was a payment of ₱1,080,402.39 from Elizabeth Mendoza leaving the outstanding Due from Officers and Employees to ₱117,635.45. No request for write-off has been made since as per COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, it is specifically stated that request for write-off shall not cover “Receivables arising from Cash Shortage” and the said outstanding Due from Officers &Employees belongs to cash shortages category. The Municipal Accountant is requesting the auditor for guidance on how to write-off those receivables especially those employees that are deceased and can no longer be traced. 
Breakdown of receivables due to shortages: Elizabeth Mendoza -₱1,134,275.59; Orlando Acero - ₱61,017.26 and Joesana Lagrimosa-₱2,744.99

6.9   We recommended and Management agreed to formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations and submit the fully accomplished AAPSI to COA within the reglementary period copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 99 of RA No. 11936.

8.10  We recommended that Management:
Strictly monitor and demand timely submission of liquidation reports and/or immediate refund of any unused balances; and
Stop granting additional cash advances to Officials and Employees, unless settlement is made on previous cash advances, in accordance with Section 89 of PD No. 1445.

10.10 We recommended and Management agreed to direct:
The Bids and Awards Committee, the Municipal Engineer and all other implementing offices of the Municipality expedite the procurement and implementation process so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay; and

After which, copies of the contracts be submitted to the Auditor within five working days from the execution for auditorial and legal review

1.	The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant an annual Schedule of the Delinquent Stallholders as at year-end; and  
 
2.	The Municipal Accountant record in the books of accounts the receivables on the market stalls rental fees based on the report forwarded by the Municipal Treasurer and the corresponding income earned therefrom.

observations.recommendation:

Require the designated Property Custodian to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid the conduct of physical count of PPE.

    (Fully Implemented in 2020)

Thereafter, cause the inventory reports to be reconciled with the reported balances of PPE accounts to establish the correct balances presented in the financial statements.
    (Fully Implemented in 2020).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require
  the BAC to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts
  and each of all the  documents  forming  part  thereof  within  five  (5)
  working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No.  2009-001  dated
  February 12, 2009.

We recommended and  the  Management  agreed  that  the  Bids  and  Awards
  Committee to comply with   the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-010  and
  ensure that  vital  information  must  be  provided  in  the  POs  before
  recommending for approval.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local  Chief  Executive
  (LCE) require the offices concerned to diligently post  and  observe  the
  timelines in the posting of  the  required  operational  reports  at  the
  designated  places  in  order  to  attain  maximum  transparency  in  LGU
  governance.

We recommend that the Municipality of Valderrama ensure that payments  of
  salaries and wages is  in  accordance  with  the  existing  Local  Budget
  Circular issued by the DBM  particularly  adopting  rates  equivalent  to
  Local Government Unit level and income classification.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

   1. Require the Municipal Engineer  together  with  Resident  Engineer  to
      closely monitor the proper implementation of the  Occupational  Safety
      and Health Standards and Programs for construction projects to  ensure
      the safety of every worker employed therein, as well  as  the  general
      public.


   2.  Ensure the inclusion of Occupational Safety and Health provisions  in
      the preparation of the Program of Works and consequently  be  included
      in the contract.

In view of the above, we recommended and the Management agreed  that  the
  Disposal Committee should take proper  action  on  government  properties
  that are for  disposal  to  prevent  their  continued  deterioration  and
  decline in value leading to loss of possible income from disposal.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer  and
  all  other  implementing  offices  of  the  Municipality   expedite   the
  implementation of all projects funded from the 20% DF to  ensure  optimum
  utilization of the fund so that the  services  and  benefits  from  these
  projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay.


  We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Local  Development
  Council conduct a careful and well planned identification of  development
  projects and choose an implementation ready PPAs to be  funded  from  the
  20% DF so the desired level of efficiency is achieved.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical count of the LGU’s PPEs pursuant to Section 102 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Section 266, Volume I of Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Assign all the collection functions to their bonded accountable officers; and 

Direct the collecting officer to immediately issue an official receipt in acknowledgment for all payments received.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare the PMR of the Municipality and submit it to the GPPB within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance to Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184 and GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

We recommended that the Management: 

Prioritize the utilization of the LDRRMF prior years’ unexpended balance; and

Revert back to the general fund any unutilized amount after five years to be programmed to other social services to be identified by the local sanggunian.

We recommended the following: 

Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance.

Make sure that the programs, projects and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness be implemented within the scheduled timeframes.

We recommended that the Management require the GAD Focal Point furnish the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report as provided for in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 in order to verify on whether gender issues contained in the GAD Plan and Budget were fully addressed.

We recommended that the Municipality through its Local Chief Executive optimally utilize the 20 percent DF by:

Instructing concerned officials to carefully evaluate the programs and projects to be appropriated in the 20 percent DF with due consideration that the same can be implemented as scheduled; and
(Fully Implemented in 2020)

Directing the involved offices to fast track the implementation of programs and projects under the 20 percent DF of prior years.
observations.reason: The 20% DP Projects for CY 2019 has one project left unimplemented. This is the Construction/ Rehabilitation of Barangay Day Center at Binanogan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management efficiently implement the planned PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan during the year to attain the objectives pursuant to NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.  Likewise, require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate the LDRRMFIP, if needed, identify the items and concerns that constraints the implementation and determine measures to attain the desired DRRM objectives

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials involved in the planning and implementation of 20 percent DF to evaluate the necessity, feasibility and timing of programs and projects implementation in the ensuing year to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  Likewise, development projects should be identified in accordance with the aforementioned JMC.
observations.reason: Purchase of relocation site for Sitio Huna, Buluangan I remained unimplemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to process the titling of the lots in the name of the local government unit.
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor had already processed three Land Titles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile and validate the outstanding receivables and determine its collectability.  If it is impossible to collect those receivables, a request for write off be made with the Commission on   Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-01.

We reiterated to recommend that Management take the following courses of action:
Require the designated Property Custodian to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid the conduct of physical count of PPE.

Conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all the properties and prepare the required RPCPPE.




Thereafter, cause the inventory reports to be reconciled with the reported balances of PPE accounts to establish the correct balances presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:

Stop the practice of paying various expenses through reimbursement basis, except for payment of travels and petty operating expenses; and














Require the Department Head to judiciously plan its procurement and ensure adherence to pertinent provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We recommended that in the forthcoming transactions of the agency, the BAC should strictly observe adherence to pertinent provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, particularly Section 47.

We recommended that the Municipality through its Local Chief Executive optimally utilize the 20 percent DF by:


Instructing concerned officials to carefully evaluate the programs and projects to be appropriated in the 20 percent DF with due consideration that the same can be implemented as scheduled; and


Directing the involved offices to fast track the implementation of programs and projects under the 20 percent DF of prior years.

We recommended that the Management efficiently implement the planned PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan during the year to attain the objectives pursuant to NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.  Likewise, require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate the LDRRMFIP, if needed, identify the items and concerns that constraints the implementation and determine measures to attain the desired DRRM objectives

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Compute the depreciation of all depreciable properties;

Make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the computation; and

Provide yearly depreciation for all depreciable properties in accordance with the above mentioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that reciprocal accounts balances are reconciled.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant work together to discuss the possible adjustments needed for reconciliation of the general ledger balances and cashbook balances of the Cash – Local Treasury and the Cash in Bank – LCCA accounts at least quarterly in accordance with Section 181 of the GAAM Vol. 1 in order to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the cash accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials involved in the planning and implementation of 20 percent DF to evaluate the necessity, feasibility and timing of programs and projects implementation in the ensuing year to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  Likewise, development projects should be identified in accordance with the aforementioned JMC.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to process the titling of the lots in the name of the local government unit.

We reiterated to recommend that Management take the following courses of action:
Require the designated Property Custodian to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid the conduct of physical count of PPE;


Conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all the properties and prepare the required RPCPPE; and


Thereafter, cause the inventory reports to be reconciled with the reported balances of PPE accounts to establish the correct balances presented in the financial statements.

        and prepare the required RPCPPE.

     c.  Thereafter, cause the inventory reports to be reconciled with  the
        reported balances of PPE accounts to establish the correct balances
        presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:

     a) Stop the practice of paying various expenses through  reimbursement
        basis, except for payment of travels and petty operating  expenses;
        and


     b) Require the Department Head to judiciously plan its procurement and
        ensure adherence to pertinent provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of  RA
        9184.

We recommended that in the forthcoming transactions of  the  agency,  the
   BAC should strictly observe adherence to  pertinent  provisions  of  2016
   Revised IRR of RA 9184, particularly Section 47.

We recommended that the Municipality through its  Local  Chief  Executive
   optimally utilize the 20 percent DF by:

     a. Instructing concerned officials to carefully evaluate the  programs
        and projects to be appropriated in  the  20  percent  DF  with  due
        consideration that the same can be implemented as scheduled; and


     b. Directing the involved offices to fast track the implementation  of
        programs and projects under the 20 percent DF of prior years.

We recommended that the Management efficiently implement the planned PPAs
   contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan during  the
   year to attain the objectives pursuant to NDRRMC, DBM and  DILG  JMC  No.
   2013-1.  Likewise, require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate the LDRRMFIP,
   if  needed,  identify  the  items  and  concerns  that  constraints   the
   implementation  and  determine  measures  to  attain  the  desired   DRRM
   objectives.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Compute the depreciation of all depreciable properties;



Make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the computation; and

Provide yearly depreciation for all depreciable properties in accordance with the above mentioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Some depreciable PPEs were not depreciated

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensures that all motor vehicles by the agency are marked, “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” and the written name and logo of the Municipality of Valderrama on each side of the government vehicle to determine ownership by the government in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: The agency managed to place proper markings on each side of the government vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that responsible officials immediately submit reports and disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate timely conduct of audit. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive supervise the implementation of those controls and do necessary action for its effective enforcement.
observations.managementAction: Submission of accounts were made within the reglementary period for CY 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared accountant’s certification as to liquidation of previous cash advance before the grant of subsequent cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that reciprocal accounts balances are reconciled.
observations.managementAction: There is a reconciliation of reciprocal accounts except for the unreconciled balance amounting to ₱721,026.79.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should refrain from allowing personnel hired under job order contracts to attend trainings and seminars to prevent incurrence of additional expenses on the part of the government.
observations.managementAction: The agency settled to refrain from sending personnel hired under job order contracts to trainings and seminars.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant work together to discuss the possible adjustments needed for reconciliation of the general ledger balances and cashbook balances of the Cash – Local Treasury and the Cash in Bank – LCCA accounts at least quarterly in accordance with Section 181 of the GAAM Vol. 1 in order to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the cash accounts.
observations.managementAction: Cash – Local Treasury is the remaining unreconciled account. Meanwhile, the account for the year 2017 onwards was already reconciled. Unreconciled account can be traced wayback 2016 backward.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials involved in the planning and implementation of 20 percent DF to evaluate the necessity, feasibility and timing of programs and projects implementation in the ensuing year to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  Likewise, development projects should be identified in accordance with the aforementioned JMC.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to process the titling of the lots in the name of the local government unit.
observations.managementAction: Initial actions were made but lacks substantial budget for titling.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated to recommend that Management take the following courses  of
   action:

     a. Require the designated  Property  Custodian  to  maintain  property
        cards and update them periodically to aid the conduct  of  physical
        count of PPE;

     b. Conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all the  properties
        and prepare the required RPCPPE; and

     c.  Thereafter, cause the inventory reports to be reconciled with  the
        reported balances of PPE accounts to establish the correct balances
        presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

     a) Compute the depreciation of all depreciable properties;

     b)  Make  the  necessary  adjustment  in  the  books  based   on   the
        computation; and

     c) Provide yearly  depreciation  for  all  depreciable  properties  in
        accordance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that management ensures that all  motor  vehicles  by  the
   agency are marked, “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” and the written name and  logo
   of the Municipality of Valderrama on each side of the government  vehicle
   to determine ownership by the government in accordance with COA  Circular
   No. 77-61.

We recommended that responsible officials immediately submit reports  and
   disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents to the Office of
   the Auditor to facilitate timely conduct of audit.  We  also  recommended
   that the Local Chief Executive  supervise  the  implementation  of  those
   controls and do necessary action for its effective enforcement.

We  recommended  that  all  claims  against  government  funds  shall  be
   supported with complete documentation in  accordance  with  COA  Circular
   2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a  detailed  review  of
   the accounts  and  determine  the  cause  of  the  unreconciled  amounts.
   Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in  the  books  of
   accounts to present  the  correct  balances  of  the  affected  accounts.
   Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s
   financial transactions to ensure that reciprocal  accounts  balances  are
   reconciled.

We recommended that Management should  refrain  from  allowing  personnel
   hired under job order contracts  to  attend  trainings  and  seminars  to
   prevent incurrence of additional expenses on the part of the government.

We reiterated to recommend that the Municipality conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all the properties and prepare the required RPCPPE.  We further recommended that the inventory reports be reconciled with the reported balances of PPE accounts to establish the correct balances presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management reassured that this will be implemented within the CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant work together to discuss the possible adjustments needed for reconciliation of the general ledger balances and cashbook balances of the Cash – Local Treasury and the Cash in Bank – LCCA accounts at least quarterly in accordance with Section 181 of the GAAM Vol. 1 in order to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the cash accounts.
observations.managementAction: Cash in Bank was already reconciled while Cash in Local Treasury still on-going its reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality hire qualified personnel to occupy the three mandatory staff position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by the IRR of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.4 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014-1, or augment the staff of the LDRRM Office based on Item 5.5 of the same JMC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality hired two full-time employees under LDRRMO on December 2018 and March 2019, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency transfer the SEF intended for sports development directly to the DepEd as a supplementary budget need for the operation of public schools, in order to subject the utilization for timely audit.  Likewise, charges against the SEF should be supported with documents and properly evaluated as to legality of the claim.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality discontinued the practice of transferring fund from SEF to any private organizations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive being the chairman of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board:
Direct the concerned employees to prepare a Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan for the conversion of the open dumpsite into a sanitary landfill pursuant to the IRR of Republic Act No. 9003;
Utilize the appropriated funds for timely completion of programs and projects supporting the conversion of open dumpsite into Sanitary Landfill; and
Request assistance from the National Solid Waste Management Commission for the products, facilities, technologies and processes to enhance proper solid waste management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials involved in the planning and implementation of 20 percent DF to evaluate the necessity, feasibility and timing of programs and projects implementation in the ensuing year to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  Likewise, development projects should be identified in accordance with the aforementioned JMC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a team to conduct the inventory of property, plant and equipment, who will prepare the required RPCPPE to be the basis for reconciliation with the accounting records.  Likewise, the conduct of physical inventory should be given attention in the incoming years in order to determine the location and actual status of the assets, whether still serviceable or already unserviceable and should be properly disposed of following the prescribed procedures.
observations.managementAction: The agency had created an Inventory Committee. However, they failed to prepare the RPCPPE for Calendar Year 2018.
observations.reason: Finding was first raised in CY 2016 AAR and reiterated in Part II – Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive designate a Disbursing Officer and direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records of the Cash – Local Treasury at least quarterly in accordance with Section 181 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Vol. 1.
observations.reason: This audit observation was first raised in CY 2013 AAR and reiterated in Part II – Finding No. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile and validate the outstanding receivables and determine its collectability.  If it is impossible to collect those receivables, a request for write off be made with the Commission on   Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-01.

We recommended that the LGU implement the infrastructure projects funded from various National Government Agencies in accordance with the parameters of the Memorandum of Agreement so that the beneficiaries can avail of its immediate benefits.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer properly plan and implement the programs/projects funded under the 20% development fund in accordance DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2005-1 dated September 20, 2005.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to process the titling of the lots in the name of the local government unit.
observations.managementAction: The titling of the lot is still in process.
observations.reason: Finding was first raised in CY 2011 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a team to conduct the inventory of property, plant and equipment, who will prepare the required RPCPPE to be the basis for reconciliation with the accounting records.  Likewise, the conduct of physical inventory should be given attention in the incoming years in order to determine the location and actual status of the assets, whether still serviceable or already unserviceable and should be properly disposed of following the prescribed procedures.

The agency had created an Inventory Committee. However, they failed to prepare the RPCPPE for Calendar Year 2017.

The finding was first raised in CY 2016 AAR and reiterated in Part II – Finding No. 1 of this report.

We reiterated to recommend  that  the  Municipality  conduct  a  complete
   physical inventory taking of all the properties and prepare the  required
   RPCPPE.  We further recommended that the inventory reports be  reconciled
   with the reported balances of  PPE  accounts  to  establish  the  correct
   balances presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal  Accountant
   work  together  to  discuss   the   possible   adjustments   needed   for
   reconciliation of the general ledger balances and  cashbook  balances  of
   the Cash – Local Treasury and the Cash in Bank – LCCA accounts  at  least
   quarterly in accordance with Section 181 of the GAAM Vol. 1 in  order  to
   ensure the accuracy and reliability of the cash accounts.

We recommended that the Municipality hire qualified personnel  to  occupy
   the three mandatory staff position of LDRRMO  following  the  composition
   and structure, position titles and salary grades set by the IRR of RA No.
   10121 and Item 5.4 of NDRRMC, DILG,  DBM  and  CSC  JMC  No.  2014-1,  or
   augment the staff of the LDRRM Office based on Item 5.5 of the same JMC.

We recommended that the agency  transfer  the  SEF  intended  for  sports
   development directly to the DepEd as a supplementary budget need for  the
   operation of public schools, in order  to  subject  the  utilization  for
   timely audit.  Likewise, charges against the SEF should be supported with
   documents and properly evaluated as to legality of the claim.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive being the chairman  of  the
   Municipal Solid Waste Management Board:

        1. Direct the concerned employees to prepare  a  Safe  Closure  and
           Rehabilitation Plan for the conversion of the open dumpsite into
           a sanitary landfill pursuant to the  IRR  of  Republic  Act  No.
           9003;


        2. Utilize the appropriated funds for timely completion of programs
           and projects supporting the conversion  of  open  dumpsite  into
           Sanitary Landfill; and


        3. Request assistance from the National Solid Waste Management
           Commission for the products, facilities, technologies and
           processes to enhance proper solid waste management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct  all  concerned  officials
   involved in the planning and implementation of 20 percent DF to  evaluate
   the  necessity,  feasibility  and  timing  of   programs   and   projects
   implementation in the  ensuing  year  to  achieve  the  desirable  socio-
   economic development as prescribed by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum  Circular
   (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  Likewise, development projects
   should be identified in accordance with the aforementioned JMC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Award Committee Chairman to strictly adhere with the required procedures of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants to promote transparency, efficiency and economy in every transaction.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor the preparation of the RAAF by the accountable officers and prepare the CRAAF for submission to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with Sections 50 and 51 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer to formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development councils.  Likewise, LDRRMFIP should be prepared as part of the LDRRMP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive execute a contract of service or job order contract with the job order employees before the start of their service.  Likewise, the contract should expressly state the lump sum work or services to be undertaken, the daily rate, duration of employment, and the other conditions required by CSC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to process transactions in accordance with governing rules and regulations on documentary requirements.  Likewise, supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers should be intact and properly evaluated before payments will be made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive designate a Disbursing Officer and direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records of the Cash – Local Treasury at least quarterly in accordance with Section 181 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Vol. 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the MTO failed to reconcile their records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile and validate the outstanding receivables and determine its collectability.  If it is impossible to collect those receivables, a request for write off be made with the Commission on   Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-01.
observations.managementAction: Part of the receivable is the subject of on-going case and write-off will be made in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU implement the infrastructure projects funded from various National Government Agencies in accordance with the parameters of the Memorandum of Agreement so that the beneficiaries can avail of its immediate benefits.
observations.managementAction:
Most of the projects were already completed but some were implemented beyond schedule.

Finding was first raised in CY 2015 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer properly plan and implement the programs/projects funded under the 20% development fund in accordance DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2005-1 dated September 20, 2005.
observations.managementAction:
Majority of the programs, projects and activities were not implemented during the year.

Finding was first raised in CY 2015 AAR and reiterated in this report under Part II – Finding No. 6.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to process the titling of the lots in the name of the local government unit
observations.managementAction:
The titling of the lot is still in process.

Finding was first raised in CY 2011 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a team to conduct the inventory of property, plant and equipment, who will prepare the required RPCPPE to be the basis for reconciliation with the accounting records.  Likewise, the conduct of physical inventory should be given attention in the incoming years in order to determine the location and actual status of the assets, whether still serviceable or already unserviceable and should be properly disposed of following the prescribed procedures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Award Committee Chairman to strictly adhere with the required procedures of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants to promote transparency, efficiency and economy in every transaction.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor the preparation of the RAAF by the accountable officers and prepare the CRAAF for submission to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with Sections 50 and 51 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer to formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development councils.  Likewise, LDRRMFIP should be prepared as part of the LDRRMP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive execute a contract of service or job order contract with the job order employees before the start of their service.  Likewise, the contract should expressly state the lump sum work or services to be undertaken, the daily rate, duration of employment, and the other conditions required by CSC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to process transactions in accordance with governing rules and regulations on documentary requirements.  Likewise, supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers should be intact and properly evaluated before payments will be made.

We recommended that management adhere to existing controls prescribed by pertinent regulations to ensure on time submission of accounts. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive supervise the implementation of those controls and do necessary action for its effective enforcement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive designate a Disbursing Officer and direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records of the Cash – Local Treasury at least quarterly in accordance with Section 181 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Vol. 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor the remittances of collections by the collectors and deposit the collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day to the authorized government depository bank in accordance with Sec. 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGU and Sec. 69 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile and validate the outstanding receivables and determine its collectability.  If it is impossible to collect those receivables, a request for write off be made with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-01.

We recommended that the Municipality of Valderrama abide with the general principles of the imprest system in granting of cash advances in accordance with Section 172(b) of GAAM, Volume 1.  Further, the amount of the cash advances that should be granted to an accountable officer be limited to her maximum accountability based on her approved fidelity bond in accordance with Section 182 of GAAM Vol. 1 as amended by Treasury Circular No.  2 - 2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipality properly plan the schedule of activities for the celebration of Panubason Festival and prepare a budget/estimate of expenses to be incurred such that the amount of cash advance intended for the activity be reasonable and based only on the budget.  We also recommended that the cash advances be liquidated in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012 – 04 dated Nov. 28, 2012.

We recommended that the LGU official exercise due care in the preparation of the Purchase Orders so that complete information are indicated therein as required in COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that the LGU implement the infrastructure projects funded from various National Government Agencies in accordance with the parameters of the Memorandum of Agreement so that the beneficiaries can avail of its immediate benefits.

We recommended that Box A of the Obligation Requests for the utilization of the Special Education Fund of the Municipality be approved/certified by the District Supervisor as co – chairperson of the Local School Board.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer properly plan and implement the programs/projects funded under the 20% development fund in accordance DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2005-1 dated September 20, 2005.

We recommend that a Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format shall be prepared and certified correct by the Local Accountant.   The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) shall submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor of the LGU.

We recommended that the GAD focal person submit the actual accomplishments vis-à-vis targets of GAD activities/programs.  We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit a certification for the total GAD expenditures in accordance with the DBM, NEDA and National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women Joint Circular No. 2004-01.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the preparation of an updated Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties and creation of a committee to take charge of the disposable procedures following Section 379 of the Local Government Code.  Unserviceable properties with salvage value be sold at public auction.  Likewise, require that the stockroom for unserviceable properties be properly secured and locked to safeguard them from probable losses.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to process the titling of the lots in the name of the local government unit
